summary: Gruber announces a discount for purchasing multiple Daring Fireball t-shirts, offering additional shirts at $20.48 each after the first at full price ($30.72). He walks readers through a manual PayPal-based ordering process since he never built a proper multi-item order form. International buyers pay a $25 per-shirt rate to cover shipping costs. The post is a straightforward logistical announcement for his merchandise operation.
key_points: Additional Daring Fireball shirts available at $20.48 each after purchasing one at $30.72
The ordering process is deliberately low-tech, using PayPal donations and manual processing
International orders carry a higher per-shirt cost ($25) to cover shipping
Buyers can optionally request additional membership keys for each extra shirt
money_quotes: I never got around to setting up a form where you can specify multiple shirt sizes, so we'll do it low-tech-style.
I can't eat the cost of overseas shipping for this price.
key_insight: A minor logistical post about Daring Fireball merchandise, notable mainly as an early example of Gruber's indie business model and his willingness to use low-tech workarounds rather than over-engineer solutions.
